tasks.title: Add client deps: pdfjs-dist, tesseract.js, jszip
tasks.description: Add the three client-side libraries Pet Health needs and confirm the app still builds. Add `pdfjs-dist` (PDF text-layer extraction), `tesseract.js` (local WASM OCR), and `jszip` (archive export/import) to the root package.json dependencies, and update package-lock.json via `npm install` at the repo root (npm workspaces). Pick versions compatible with Next.js 16.2 / React 19. Do NOT write any feature code — this task only lands the dependencies so later tasks don't conflict on package.json/lockfile. Read AGENTS.md: this is a customized Next.js; do not assume upstream behavior.
acceptance: pdfjs-dist, tesseract.js, and jszip are present in package.json dependencies
npm install completes and package-lock.json is updated
npm run build still succeeds with the new deps installed

tasks.title: Add pet-health and ai-provider storage keys
tasks.description: Extend the STORAGE_KEYS map in src/core/storage/keys.ts with two new keys, following the existing string-literal pattern: `petHealth: "tool:pet-health"` (the tool's state slice) and `aiProvider: "hub:ai-provider"` (the hub-level AI provider config + key, treated like the existing OAuth token keys). Do not change CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION or DB_VERSION. Per-file record Blobs will use dynamically-built keys of the form `tool:pet-health:file:{id}` and do not need an entry here. This is an additive, mechanical change matching the surrounding entries.
acceptance: STORAGE_KEYS gains petHealth = 'tool:pet-health'
STORAGE_KEYS gains aiProvider = 'hub:ai-provider'
Existing keys and exports are unchanged; build passes
tasks.modelReason: haiku — add two typed keys to an existing const map, explicit pattern shown in the feature/affected-areas

tasks.title: Pet Health data-model types
tasks.description: Create the TypeScript types for the Pet Health state slice under src/tools/pet-health/types/. Define: (1) Pet — id, name (required), species (required), breed?, dateOfBirth?, sex?, weight?, microchipId?, vetName?, clinic?, notes?, createdAt, updatedAt. (2) Record (medical record / sign-out doc) — id, petId, title, documentType ('medical-record'|'discharge'|'lab'|'imaging'|'invoice'|'other'), documentDate?, source?, fileName, mimeType, fileSize, uploadedAt, updatedAt, fileRef (id used for the per-file Blob key), extractedText?, extractionStatus ('pending'|'extracting'|'done'|'empty'|'failed'), extractionMethod ('pdf-text'|'ocr'|'vision'|'none'). (3) Conversation/ChatMessage — per-pet message history: messages of role 'user'|'assistant' with content and a timestamp, keyed by petId. (4) PetHealthState — schemaVersion (number), pets: Pet[], records: Record[], conversations: Record-by-petId message arrays, plus any durability metadata (e.g. lastFolderName?, persistedRequestedAt?). Also create src/tools/pet-health/types/ai.ts: AiProvider = 'openai'|'anthropic'; AiProviderConfig — provider, apiKey, model. Keep these as pure type/interface declarations; no logic. Restate from the feature files: only name and species are required on a Pet; records carry both file metadata and extracted text.
acceptance: Pet, Record, Conversation/ChatMessage, and PetHealthState types exist with the fields above
PetHealthState carries schemaVersion
AiProvider and AiProviderConfig types exist (openai | anthropic, plus model)
Types compile (npm run build)
tasks.modelReason: sonnet — defines the tool's data model, some design judgment shaping fields consumed by every later task

tasks.title: Pet Health storage layer (state slice + per-file Blob store)
tasks.description: Implement persistence for Pet Health following the existing room-coat/home-maintenance storage pattern (src/tools/*/storage). Create src/tools/pet-health/storage/index.ts with: loadPetHealth() — read STORAGE_KEYS.petHealth via core storage getItem, seed a fresh normalized default if absent, normalize/migrate on load (handle schemaVersion), and always write the current schemaVersion; savePetHealth(state); and importPetHealthSlice(data) — normalize unknown input into a valid PetHealthState (used by the export/import bundle). Create src/tools/pet-health/storage/files.ts — a per-file Blob store keyed `tool:pet-health:file:{fileRef}` using the core IndexedDB kv API: putRecordFile(fileRef, Blob), getRecordFile(fileRef): Promise<Blob|undefined>, deleteRecordFile(fileRef). Keep the JSON slice free of Blobs (only metadata + fileRef + extractedText live in the slice). Use the shared createId() helper for ids. Restate: deleting a record must remove both its metadata and its Blob; deleting a pet cascades to all its records' Blobs — expose helpers the provider can call.
acceptance: loadPetHealth/savePetHealth persist the slice under tool:pet-health and normalize+migrate on load with schemaVersion
importPetHealthSlice normalizes arbitrary input to a valid state (seeds default when absent)
Per-file Blob store reads/writes/deletes Blobs under tool:pet-health:file:{fileRef}
Slice stays JSON-serializable (no Blobs inside it)
tasks.modelReason: sonnet — follows an established storage pattern; the per-file Blob store is a small new but well-scoped addition

tasks.title: AI provider config storage (hub:ai-provider)
tasks.description: Implement load/save/clear for the AI provider configuration under STORAGE_KEYS.aiProvider ('hub:ai-provider'), separate from the pet-health slice and treated like OAuth tokens. Create src/tools/pet-health/storage/ai-config.ts: loadAiConfig(): Promise<AiProviderConfig|null>, saveAiConfig(config), clearAiConfig(), and isAiConfigured(config): boolean (true when provider is known and apiKey is non-empty). Provide sensible default model constants per provider (latest capable defaults — read the claude-api skill for the current Anthropic model id; pick the current capable OpenAI chat model). This module only persists/reads config; no network calls. Important: this key must NOT be included in the export bundle (enforced in T-011).
acceptance: AI config persists under hub:ai-provider, separate from the tool slice
load/save/clear and isAiConfigured work
Sensible latest-capable default model per provider is provided
tasks.modelReason: sonnet — small secret-handling persistence module; correctness of the exclude-from-export contract matters

tasks.title: Text-extraction libs (PDF text layer + local Tesseract OCR)
tasks.description: Implement in-browser, no-network text extraction. Create src/tools/pet-health/lib/extract-pdf.ts using pdfjs-dist to pull the embedded text layer from a PDF Blob, concatenating pages with page markers; configure the pdf.js worker correctly for this CUSTOMIZED Next.js 16 build (read node_modules/next/dist/docs as AGENTS.md requires; keep it client-side). Create src/tools/pet-health/lib/extract-ocr.ts using tesseract.js to OCR an image Blob (and rasterized scanned-PDF pages) entirely locally — no key, no network. Create src/tools/pet-health/lib/extract.ts — an orchestrator extractRecordText(blob, mimeType) that: for PDFs tries the text layer first and falls back to OCR when the text layer is empty; for images uses OCR; returns { text, method: 'pdf-text'|'ocr'|'none', status: 'done'|'empty'|'failed' }. Must run async without blocking the UI and be idempotent (re-running replaces prior output). Do not wire into the provider/UI here — just the libs. v1 has NO vision-model path.
acceptance: Digital PDFs: embedded text extracted in-browser, multi-page concatenated with page markers
Scanned PDFs/images: local Tesseract.js OCR fallback, no network/key
Orchestrator returns text + method + status and is idempotent
pdf.js worker is configured so npm run build succeeds (no SSR/worker breakage)
tasks.modelReason: opus — pdf.js worker + Tesseract WASM bundling in a customized Next build is fiddly and unfamiliar; wrong setup breaks the build

tasks.title: Chat libs: system prompt, history trim, OpenAI + Anthropic adapters
tasks.description: Implement the provider-agnostic chat plumbing as pure libs (no React). (1) src/tools/pet-health/lib/build-system-prompt.ts — buildSystemPrompt(pet, records) producing a system message containing the pet profile fields plus the concatenated extractedText of that pet's records, each under a header with the record title/date/type; include the non-clinical disclaimer instruction. (2) src/tools/pet-health/lib/trim-history.ts — trimContext({systemPrompt, history, newMessage}, budget) using a char-based token estimate (~4 chars/token), reserving a portion of the model context for the reply, trimming history oldest-first, then truncating record context with a flag when still over; return the messages to send plus a `truncated` boolean. (3) src/tools/pet-health/lib/providers/openai.ts and anthropic.ts — each exposes sendChat(config, messages): Promise<{text}> calling the provider's HTTP API directly from the browser with the user's key (OpenAI Chat Completions shape; Anthropic Messages shape with system separate from messages). READ THE claude-api SKILL before writing the Anthropic adapter (correct endpoint, headers incl. anthropic-version and the browser-access header, model id, request/response shape). (4) providers/index.ts — sendChat(config, ...) dispatching by provider. Non-streaming. Surface readable errors for invalid key / rate limit so the UI can show + offer retry.
acceptance: System prompt includes pet profile + concatenated record extractedText with per-record headers + disclaimer
History trimmed oldest-first to a token budget; record context truncated with a flag when needed
OpenAI and Anthropic adapters call their APIs directly with the user's key (correct shapes); dispatch by provider
Provider/quota/auth errors are surfaced as readable, retryable errors
tasks.modelReason: opus — two distinct provider API integrations (incl. Anthropic, must follow claude-api skill) plus token budgeting; correctness-critical

tasks.title: PetHealthProvider (pets, records, conversations state)
tasks.description: Create src/tools/pet-health/PetHealthProvider.tsx — the React context provider that owns Pet Health state, following the HomeMaintenanceProvider/RoomCoatProvider pattern. On mount, load the slice (T-005) and expose isReady. Expose mutations that persist via savePetHealth and survive reload: pets — createPet, updatePet, deletePet (cascades to that pet's records + their Blobs via the files store), plus active/selected pet tracking; records — addRecord(petId, File) which stores the Blob (files store), creates the metadata record, then asynchronously runs extraction (T-007 extract orchestrator) updating extractionStatus/extractedText on completion (idempotent re-extract action), updateRecord (metadata), deleteRecord (removes metadata + Blob), getRecordFile (for open/download); conversations — getMessages(petId), appendMessage(petId, msg), clearConversation(petId); deleting a pet clears its conversation. Keep network/provider-call logic OUT of here (the chat UI calls the chat libs and then appendMessage). Expose a typed usePetHealth() hook; throw if used outside the provider. Read AGENTS.md / Next docs for the client-component boundary.
acceptance: Provider loads the slice, exposes isReady, and a usePetHealth() hook
Pet CRUD + active-pet selection; deleting a pet cascades to records, Blobs, and conversation
Record add stores Blob + metadata then runs extraction async (idempotent re-extract); update/delete work
Conversation get/append/clear persist; all mutations survive reload
tasks.modelReason: opus — central shared-state architecture consumed by every UI task; high blast radius, orchestrates storage + async extraction

tasks.title: Durable-storage libs (persistent storage, FS-Access folder, archive)
tasks.description: Implement the durability mechanisms as libs (no React). (1) src/tools/pet-health/lib/persistent-storage.ts — requestPersistentStorage() wrapping navigator.storage.persist()/persisted(), degrading gracefully when unavailable; return the resulting state. (2) src/tools/pet-health/lib/fs-folder.ts — File System Access API: feature-detect support; connectFolder() (showDirectoryPicker) returning a handle; writeAll(handle, state, getBlob) writing each record's original file into the folder organized per pet plus a self-describing JSON index pet-health.json (pets + record metadata + extractedText); readIndex(handle) to reconcile on reconnect (folder is source of truth for file bytes; metadata merged last-write-wins by updatedAt). Persist the handle if practical, else require reconnect. (3) src/tools/pet-health/lib/archive.ts — using jszip, exportArchive(state, getBlob): Blob (zip of original files + index) and importArchive(zip): { state, files } as the fallback for browsers without FS Access. All operations are local/no-network. Read node_modules/next docs re: keeping these browser-only (no SSR).
acceptance: requestPersistentStorage() calls navigator.storage.persist() and degrades gracefully
FS-Access: connect folder, write per-pet files + self-describing pet-health.json index, reconcile on reconnect
Where FS-Access is unavailable, jszip archive export/import bundles original files + index
All paths are local; build succeeds with these browser-only modules
tasks.modelReason: opus — File System Access + zip archiving is unfamiliar, browser-variant, and easy to get subtly wrong

Mirror the existing per-tool import wiring used by the settings import flow.

AI provider key and OAuth tokens are excluded from the bundle
isNexusExportBundle accepts versions 1/2/3; v1 and v2 bundles import successfully seeding absent slices
pet-health slice carries metadata + extracted text only (no raw Blobs)

tasks.title: Settings page: AI provider + durability
tasks.description: Build the tool's settings UI at src/app/tools/pet-health/settings/page.tsx with two sections. (A) AI provider (FEAT-pet-health-4): choose provider (openai|anthropic), enter a masked API key, optionally choose a model (default pre-filled per provider); save via saveAiConfig (T-006), show a 'configured' state (provider + masked key + model) with a Clear action; never render the raw key back in full; minimal validation (non-empty key, known provider). Expose/compose an availability gate lib src/tools/pet-health/lib/ai-availability.ts (or reuse isAiConfigured) that the chat reads. (B) Durability (FEAT-pet-health-6): a button to request persistent storage (T-010), a 'Connect document folder' control where File System Access is supported (hidden/disabled otherwise) that connects + writes-through the current state via fs-folder, and an 'Export archive' action using archive.ts as the fallback; show the current durability posture (persistent? folder connected? last archive export?). Wire a write-through so record/pet changes are reflected to a connected folder (an effect at this level or via provider subscription — keep it simple, last-write-wins). Components: src/tools/pet-health/components/AiSettings.tsx, StorageSettings.tsx. Consume usePetHealth() + the storage/durable libs.
acceptance: AI settings: pick provider, masked key entry, optional model, save/clear, configured state never shows the full key
Availability gate derives 'AI ready' from provider + non-empty key
Durability: request persistent storage, connect folder (where supported) with write-through, export archive fallback, show posture
No key/folder required for the rest of the tool to work

tasks.title: Pet chat UI (context-grounded, gated, with disclaimer)
tasks.description: Build the per-pet chat UI. Add a chat route/section under src/app/tools/pet-health (scoped to the active pet) and components src/tools/pet-health/components/ChatPanel.tsx, ChatMessage.tsx. Behavior: if AI is not configured (availability gate from T-014), gate the chat with a prompt to open Settings — do not attempt a call. When configured: render the per-pet conversation from provider.getMessages; on send, build the system prompt (buildSystemPrompt with the pet + its records), trim to budget (trim-history), call providers.sendChat(config, messages) (T-008), then appendMessage for both the user and assistant turns; while awaiting, disable input and show a processing indicator (non-streaming). Show a visible 'some records truncated' notice when trim flags truncation. Surface readable provider/network/quota errors and allow retry without corrupting stored history. Persist history per pet (provider) across reload; offer 'Clear conversation'. Show a persistent non-clinical disclaimer ('Informational only — not veterinary advice. Consult your vet.'). Consume usePetHealth() + loadAiConfig + chat libs. Read AGENTS.md / Next docs for client boundaries.
acceptance: Chat gated with a configure-key prompt when AI is not configured (no call attempted)
Each send uses pet profile + record context, trims to budget, routes to the configured provider, and persists both turns per pet
Processing indicator + disabled input while awaiting; truncation notice shown when applicable
Provider errors are readable and retryable without corrupting history; persistent non-clinical disclaimer shown; clear-conversation works
tasks.modelReason: sonnet — stateful UI, but the hard logic (prompt build, trim, provider calls) lives in already-built libs; this wires them
